entries.definition:
كتاب النون

نظر - نعف

وأنْظَمَتِ الدّجاجةُ: صار في جَوفها بَيض. ويقال لكواكب الجوزاء: نَظمٌ. وجاءنا نَظْمٌ من جَرادٍ: أي كَثير.

(نظر) النون والظاء والراء أصلٌ صحيح يرجع فروعُه إلى معنىً واحد وهو تأمُّلُ الشّيءِ ومعاينتُه، ثم يُستعار ويُتَّسَع فيه. فيقال: نظرت إلى الشّيءِ أنظُر إليه، إذا عاينْتَه. وحَيٌّ حِلاَلٌ نَظَرٌ: متجاوِرون ينظرُ بعضُهم إلى بعض. ويقولون: نَظَرتُه، أي انتظرته. وهو ذلك القياس، كأنَّه ينظر إلى الوقت الذي يأتي فيه. قال:

من الدَّهر ينفَعْني لدى أمِّ جُندَبِ

فإنّكُما إن تَنْظُرَانِيَ ليلةً

ومن باب المجاز والاتِّساع قولُهم: نظَرَتِ الأرضُ: أرَتْ نَباتَها . وهذا هو [القياس. و] يقولون: نَظَرَت بعَينٍ. ومنه نَظَرَ الدهرُ إلى بني فلانٍ فأهلكَهم. [و] هذا نظيرُ هذا، من هذا القياس؛ أي إنَّه إذا نُظِرَ إليه وإلى نَظِيرِهِ كانا سواءً. وبه نَظْرَةٌ، أي شُحوب، كأنَّه شيءٌ نُظِرَ إليه فشَحَب لونُه. والله أعلمُ بالصَّواب.

(باب النون والعين وما يثلثهما)

يَنْطِق yanṭiq [imperf. of v. نَطَق naṭaqa, intrans.] to speak (37:92)
ما لكم لا تنطقون what is [the matter] with you [that] you don’t
speak?; *(45:29) يَنْطِقُ عَلَيْكُمْ testifies against you.

أَنْطَق ʾanṭaqa [v. IV trans.] to cause to speak, to enable to
speak, to give the faculty of speech to (41:21) أَنْطَقَنَا اللَّهُ الَّذِي أَنْطَقَ كُلَّ
شَيْءٍ God, who gave speech to everything, has given us speech.

مَنْطِق manṭiq [v. n./n.] speech, language, idiom (27:16) عُلِّمْنَا مَنْطِقَ
الطَّيْرِ we have been taught the speech of birds.
entries.definition_english:

ما لكم لا تنطقون what is [the matter] with you [that] you don’t

speak, to give the faculty of speech to (41:21) أَنْطَقَنَا اللَّهُ الَّذِي أَنْطَقَ كُلَّ

الطَّيْرِ we have been taught the speech of birds.

entries.definition:
n–ẓ–r eyesight, a sight, to look at, to glimpse, to see, to watch;
evil eye; to contemplate; to compare, to debate, to be equal; to
wait, to postpone, to delay; to expect, expectation. Of this root, 10
forms occur 129 times in the Qur’an: نَظَر naẓara 87 times; أَنْظَرَ
ʾanẓara six times; يُنْظَرُون yunẓarūn six times; يَنْتَظِر yantaẓir eight
times; نَظَر naẓar once; نَاظِرُون nāẓirūn five times; نَاظِرَة nāẓiratun
twice; نَظْرَة naẓratun twice; مُنْظَرُون munẓarūn six times and مُنْتَظِرُون
muntaẓirūn six times.

نَظَر naẓara a I [v. intrans.] 1 to look on, to watch impassively
(2:50) وَأَغْرَقْنَا آلَ فِرْعَوْنَ وَأَنْتُمْ تَنْظُرُونَ and We drowned Pharaoh’s
people while you looked on 2 to look at (7:198) وَتَرَاهُمْ يَنْظُرُونَ إِلَيْكَ
and you may observe them looking at you, but they
do not see 3 [with prep. في] to examine, to scrutinise (37:88) فَنَظَرَ
نَظْرَةً فِي النُّجُومِ then he took a [careful scrutinising] look at the stars
II [v. trans] 1 to consider, to contemplate (59:18) وَلْتَنْظُرْ نَفْسٌ مَا قَدَّمَتْ
لِغَدٍ and let [every] soul consider carefully what it sends ahead for
tomorrow 2 to look (out) for, to await, to wait for (47:18) فَهَلْ
يَنْظُرُونَ إِلَّا السَّاعَةَ أَنْ تَأْتِيَهُمْ بَغْتَةً do they look for [anything] but the
Hour that will come upon them suddenly? 3 to see, to ascertain, to
evaluate (27:27) سَنَنْظُرُ أَصَدَقْتَ أَمْ كُنْتَ مِنَ الْكَاذِبِينَ we will ascertain
whether you have told the truth, or if you are one of the liars 4 to
see to find out, to discover (27:41) نَنْظُرْ أَتَهْتَدِي أَمْ تَكُونُ
مِنَ الَّذِينَ لَا يَهْتَدُونَ disguise her throne for her, that we see whether
she finds guidance or will be one of those who do not find it.

أَنْظَرَ ʾanẓara [v. IV trans.] to delay, to grant respite (38:79) رَبِّ
فَأَنْظِرْنِي إِلَى يَوْمِ يُبْعَثُونَ my Lord, grant me respite until the Day they

948

n–ẓ–r eyesight, a sight, to look at, to glimpse, to see, to watch;
evil eye; to contemplate; to compare, to debate, to be equal; to
wait, to postpone, to delay; to expect, expectation. Of this root, 10
forms occur 129 times in the Qur’an: نَظَر naẓara 87 times; أَنْظَرَ
ʾanẓara six times; يُنْظَرُون yunẓarūn six times; يَنْتَظِر yantaẓir eight
times; نَظَر naẓar once; نَاظِرُون nāẓirūn five times; نَاظِرَة nāẓiratun
twice; نَظْرَة naẓratun twice; مُنْظَرُون munẓarūn six times and مُنْتَظِرُون
muntaẓirūn six times.

people while you looked on 2 to look at (7:198) وَتَرَاهُمْ يَنْظُرُونَ إِلَيْكَ
and you may observe them looking at you, but they
do not see 3 [with prep. في] to examine, to scrutinise (37:88) فَنَظَرَ
نَظْرَةً فِي النُّجُومِ then he took a [careful scrutinising] look at the stars

لِغَدٍ and let [every] soul consider carefully what it sends ahead for

يَنْظُرُونَ إِلَّا السَّاعَةَ أَنْ تَأْتِيَهُمْ بَغْتَةً do they look for [anything] but the
Hour that will come upon them suddenly? 3 to see, to ascertain, to
evaluate (27:27) سَنَنْظُرُ أَصَدَقْتَ أَمْ كُنْتَ مِنَ الْكَاذِبِينَ we will ascertain
whether you have told the truth, or if you are one of the liars 4 to
see to find out, to discover (27:41) نَنْظُرْ أَتَهْتَدِي أَمْ تَكُونُ
مِنَ الَّذِينَ لَا يَهْتَدُونَ disguise her throne for her, that we see whether
she finds guidance or will be one of those who do not find it.

أَنْظَرَ ʾanẓara [v. IV trans.] to delay, to grant respite (38:79) رَبِّ
فَأَنْظِرْنِي إِلَى يَوْمِ يُبْعَثُونَ my Lord, grant me respite until the Day they

948

entries.definition:
shall be resurrected.
يُنْظَرُون yunẓarūn [pass. imperf.] to be granted respite, to be
reprieved, to be delayed (21:40) بَلْ تَأْتِيهِمْ بَغْتَةً فَتَبْهَتُهُمْ فَلَا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ رَدَّهَا
وَلَا هُمْ يُنْظَرُونَ but it will come upon them suddenly, and it will
stupefy them; so they will not be able to repel it nor shall they be
granted respite.

يَنْتَظِر yantaẓir [imperf. v. VIII trans.] to wait for, to await
(10:102) فَهَلْ يَنْتَظِرُونَ إِلَّا مِثْلَ أَيَّامِ الَّذِينَ خَلَوْا مِنْ قَبْلِهِمْ do they wait but for
the like of such days of [punishment as befell] those before them?

نَظَر naẓar [v. n./n.] the act of looking, beholding (47:20) يَنْظُرُونَ
إِلَيْكَ نَظَرَ الْمَغْشِيِّ عَلَيْهِ مِنَ الْمَوْتِ looking at you [Prophet] with the look
of one fainting because of [the approach of] death.

نَاظِرَة nāẓiratun [fem. act. part.; pl. of masc. act. part. نَاظِرُون
nāẓirūn] 1 beholder, onlooker, one who sees (7:108) وَنَزَعَ يَدَهُ فَإِذَا هِيَ
بَيْضَاءُ لِلنَّاظِرِينَ and then he pulled out his hand and–lo–it was white
for all to see 2 ones who contemplate (15:16) وَلَقَدْ جَعَلْنَا فِي السَّمَاءِ
بُرُوجًا وَزَيَّنَّاهَا لِلنَّاظِرِينَ We have set constellations up in the sky and
made them beautiful for those who contemplate [also: for the
beholders] 3 one waiting for, awaiting (33:53) يَاأَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَا
تَدْخُلُوا بُيُوتَ النَّبِيِّ إِلَّا أَنْ يُؤْذَنَ لَكُمْ إِلَى طَعَامٍ غَيْرَ نَاظِرِينَ إِنَاهُ you who believe,
do not enter the Prophet’s apartments, unless you are given
permission for a meal, and without waiting for its time 4 one who
sees, one who discovers, one who finds out (27:35) وَإِنِّي مُرْسِلَةٌ إِلَيْهِمْ
بِهَدِيَّةٍ فَنَاظِرَةٌ بِمَ يَرْجِعُ الْمُرْسَلُونَ but I am sending them a present, and I
will see with what the envoys return.

نَظْرَة naẓratun I [unit n.] a single glance, a look (37:88) فَنَظَرَ
نَظْرَةً then he took a [careful] look at [scrutinising] the stars
II [v. n.] the act of delaying, granting respite (2:280) وَإِنْ كَانَ ذُو
عُسْرَةٍ فَنَظِرَةٌ إِلَى مَيْسَرَةٍ but if he [the debtor] is in strained financial
circumstances, then [there should be] a delaying [of
demands/repayment] until a time of ease.

مُنْظَرُون munẓarūn [pl. of pass. part. مُنْظَر munẓar] one granted
respite, delayed (15:37) قَالَ فَإِنَّكَ مِنَ الْمُنْظَرِينَ He said, ‘You are one
of those given respite.’

949
entries.definition_english:
shall be resurrected.
